waiting_for: Pull‑back to EMA21 (~29815) for a long EMA‑bounce entry; break above 30000 for a short level‑rejection entry.

long_assessment.reasoning: Price is in a strong up‑trend (EMA21 > EMA75 > EMA150). A pull‑back to EMA21 offers a classic EMA‑bounce long. Confluence: (1) EMA21 sits ~174 pts below current price, acting as dynamic support; (2) Stochastic at 83.4 is high but not yet overbought enough to invalidate bullish bias; (3) Psychological round number 30000 lies just 10.8 pts above price – a natural target. With a 40‑pt stop below EMA21 the R/R exceeds 4:1, meeting all criteria.

short_assessment.reasoning: Stochastic is overbought (83.4) suggesting a possible mean‑reversion short. The nearest resistance is the psychological level 30000. A short would be placed as a stop order just above 30000, betting on a rejection. No bearish FVG is nearby and EMA21 is below price, so we lack a strong structural short bias. We wait for price to break above 30000 before the entry becomes valid.
